summary: I think when the Control-C is pressed you should exit all sessions but you should still keep metadata on those sessions ...

prompt.text: I think when the Control-C is pressed you should exit all sessions but you should still keep metadata on those sessions so if the daemon is relaunched immediately it can pick up where it left off

response.text:
Understood. Ctrl+C ends all active sessions, and we persist enough metadata so a restarted daemon can resume. Here’s the final execution plan with that requirement baked in.

Step-by-step execution plan (final)

Step 1) Ctrl+C ends all sessions + persistence
- Detect `0x03` in stdin thread.
- Mark all active sessions as ended (`explicit_end = true`, `signal = "CTRL+C"`).
- Persist registry state to disk so a restarted daemon can resume.
- Update help/README to reference Ctrl+C.
Files: `src/daemon.rs`, `src/cli.rs`, `README.md`, `src/session.rs` (if new structs)

Step 2) Unified session registry (multi-session + persistence)
- Replace single `ActiveSession` with a registry keyed by `(tool, session_id)`.
- Track: `root`, `last_activity_ts`, `explicit_end`, `soft_end`, `active`.
- Load registry on daemon start; save on updates and on Ctrl+C.
Files: `src/daemon.rs`, `src/session.rs`

Step 3) Review UI defaults + actions
- Enter = accept as‑is.
- `s` = squash (lowercase).
- Arrows + j/k + space remain; no gg/G.
Files: `src/status.rs`

Step 4) Remove risky squash/amend behavior
- Amend targets selected commit; refuse if selection doesn’t include HEAD.
- Squash requires contiguous selection including HEAD; use first commit summary as message (no auto “Squash N commits”).
Files: `src/status.rs`, `src/git.rs`

Step 5) Push policy fix
- Replace force push with `git push`.
- Gated by remote exists + working tree clean.
Files: `src/status.rs`, `src/git.rs`

Step 6) Cursor explicit end signal
- On `status == "completed"`, emit explicit end event (`meta.end = true`).
Files: `src/cursor.rs`

Step 7) OpenCode explicit end + timeout (600s)
- Detect `/exit` in user prompt → explicit end.
- Add inactivity timeout for OpenCode only:
  - default 600s
  - env override `GG_OPENCODE_TIMEOUT_SECS`
  - soft end (not explicit).
Files: `src/opencode.rs`, `schemas/opencode.md`, `src/daemon.rs`

Step 8) “Status can be revoked” behavior
- If a session marked ended (soft or explicit) receives new events:
  - mark active again
  - `gg status` must block review until it ends again.
Files: `src/daemon.rs`, `src/status.rs`, `src/git.rs` (session end checks)

Step 9) Documentation refresh
- Remove legacy `gg session …` references.
- Update usage: `gg`, `gg status`, `gg "<prompt>"`.
- Update Ctrl+C guidance.
Files: `README.md`, `src/cli.rs`

Notes on persistence
- Store registry state under `.git/gg/daemon.json` (or similar) so it survives daemon restarts per repo.
- On daemon start: load state, mark sessions as active unless explicitly ended and no new events have arrived.

If you want, I can turn each step into per‑file edit instructions with exact function names and data structures (still read‑only, no changes).

meta.session_title: Plan for a Git-based autonomous CLI daemon with commit automation
